title: Prohibitions of Ihram
description: Matters from which the pilgrim is barred after entering the state of devotion until exiting it, with evidences and notes.
warning_title: Important Shariah Notice for the Pilgrim
warning_body: Whoever commits a prohibition of Ihram intentionally, knowing the ruling, and by choice, has sinned, and some prohibitions require a ransom (fidya) depending on the type. However, whoever falls into the prohibition out of forgetfulness, ignorance, or coercion, this issue has details that differ based on the type of prohibition.

p1.title: Hair Removal
p1.body: It is forbidden for a person in Ihram to remove hair from their head or body intentionally by shaving, plucking, or other means, except for a Shariah excuse, such as an injury that necessitates hair removal.
p1.daleel: “And do not shave your heads until the sacrificial animal has reached its place of slaughter.” [Al-Baqarah: 196]
p1.note: If hair falls out unintentionally during wudu, bathing, or combing, there is no harm in that.

p2.title: Clipping Nails
p2.body: It is forbidden for a person in Ihram to intentionally clip the fingernails or toenails until they exit their Ihram.
p2.note: However, if a nail breaks and causes pain, there is no harm in removing the painful part.

p3.title: Using Perfume
p3.body: It is forbidden for a person in Ihram to use perfume after assuming Ihram on their body, clothing, or head. This includes: perfumes, oud oil, musk, and incense for the purpose of perfuming.
p3.note: As for perfume applied before assuming Ihram, it does not harm even if its trace remains after Ihram; because the Prophet ﷺ perfumed himself before assuming Ihram.

p4.title: Wearing Tailored Clothes (For Men)
p4.body: It is forbidden for a male pilgrim in Ihram to wear anything fitted to the body or part of it, such as shirts, pants, turbans, hooded cloaks, socks, and closed shoes.
p4.note: He is allowed to wear sandals; if sandals are not found, he may wear slippers. As for women, they do not have a specific dress for Ihram, and they wear whatever covering, non-revealing clothes they wish.

p5.title: Covering the Head (For Men)
p5.body: It is forbidden for men to cover their heads with anything directly touching it, such as caps, ghutras, or turbans.
p5.note: Seeking shade under an umbrella, a car roof, or a tent is permissible; because it is not considered covering the head in the forbidden sense.

p6.title: Wearing Niqab & Gloves (For Women)
p6.body: A woman must not wear a niqab (face veil) or gloves while in Ihram.
p6.note: If she is in the presence of non-mahram men, it is prescribed for her to draw her headscarf over her face without wearing a niqab.

p7.title: Marriage Contract
p7.body: It is not permissible for a person in Ihram to marry or marry off others, nor to propose to a woman until they exit Ihram.

p8.title: Sexual Intercourse & Its Preludes
p8.body: Sexual intercourse is one of the gravest prohibitions of Ihram. Similarly, anything leading to it, such as foreplay, kissing, or touching with desire, is forbidden.

p9.title: Hunting Land Game
p9.body: It is forbidden for a person in Ihram to hunt permissible land animals, assist in hunting them, or point them out.
p9.note: Sea fishing is not included in this ruling.

faqs.q: Is it permissible to use soap or shampoo?
faqs.a: It is permissible to use soap and shampoo if they are unscented. If they contain perfume meant for scenting, they should be avoided during Ihram.

faqs.q: Is it permissible to wear a watch or glasses?
faqs.a: Yes, it is permissible to wear a watch, glasses, earbuds, belt, fitness tracker, and carry and use a phone; because they are not among the prohibitions of Ihram.

faqs.q: Is it permissible to use an umbrella?
faqs.a: Yes, it is permissible to seek shade under an umbrella, a car roof, or the umbrellas in the Grand Mosque, and it does not affect the Ihram.

faqs.q: Is it permissible to change Ihram clothes?
faqs.a: It is permissible for the pilgrim to change their Ihram clothes if they become dirty or if they need other clothes, and they can also be washed.

faqs.q: Is it permissible to take a bath while in Ihram?
faqs.a: It is permissible for a person in Ihram to take a bath, wash their head, and perform wudu, as long as they do not intentionally remove hair.
